sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a 4-month history of back pain that radiates to the left leg, describing the pain as a 6 out of 10 in severity, but as high as 13 out of 10 at its worst.
The pain is exacerbated by standing, walking, coughing, and sneezing, but is somewhat alleviated by lying down.
The patient has difficulty standing, walking, and has a dragging sensation in the foot when walking, necessitating the use of a cane.
The patient reports a history of a fall 30 years ago that may have resulted in a fracture, but the exact nature of this event is unclear.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of back pain and leg pain, with a recent MRI showing a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae with nerve root impingement.
Review of systems: The patient reports numbness and tingling in the lower extremities, but no weakness.
The patient's back pain and leg pain are the primary concerns.
Current medications: NOT MENTIONED
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure, pulse rate, oxygen saturation, and temperature are not mentioned.

Physical exam: The patient's back exam is notable for tenderness to palpation at the L4 vertebrae, pain with flexion and extension of the back, and 4 out of 5 strength in the left leg and 5 out of 5 strength in the right leg.
Reflexes are brisk and motor and sensory function is intact in both lower extremities.
Test Results: MRI results show a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae with nerve root impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement, causing lower back pain and radiating pain to the left leg.
Problem list: Back pain; leg pain; disk herniation at L4-L5; nerve root impingement; weakness in the left leg.
Plan:
I recommended an epidural steroid injection to alleviate the inflammation and pain.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the affected area.
The patient will see physical therapy and return in one month for a follow-up appointment.
Follow-up: The patient will return in one month for a follow-up appointment.
